// purpose: IDE config for brainkit's ambient runtime type definitions.
// why-no-dom: brainkit deployments run inside a SES Compartment that ships its own polyfills (Audio / WebSocket / FormData / fetch / Response / Request / Headers / URL / …). globals.d.ts declares every one of them. Including the default DOM lib would double-declare and pollute completion with thousands of HTMLElement-family types that .ts code running inside brainkit can't use.
// why-skipLibCheck: Some ambient classes (Blob, File) exist in DOM too — users who opt into `lib: ["dom"]` on their own tsconfig get a duplicate-identifier warning that skipLibCheck suppresses. brainkit's own d.ts set is clean without DOM.
